req.title: Allergy intolerance (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers AllergyIntolerance creation with conflict detection on MedicationRequest. I selected: (1) a positive EP test that creates a valid allergy and verifies alert regeneration on a conflicting MedicationRequest; (2) a NEG test for duplicate-allergy submission expecting HTTP 409; (3) a NEG test for missing mandatory 'code' element expecting HTTP 422; (4) a STC test covering the active→resolved state transition and verifying that a resolved allergy no longer triggers an alert; (5) a BVA test posting a code string at exactly 1 character (minimal boundary) to probe field-length validation. Decision Table coverage is embedded in the STC and NEG cases by enumerating condition combinations (role, state, payload completeness).

tests.name: test_create_allergy_and_medication_conflict_alert
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient resource exists, ID available via <patient_id> fixture
Medication resource for penicillin exists, ID available via <penicillin_med_id> fixture
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with valid penicillin allergy linked to patient
POST /MedicationRequest prescribing penicillin for the same patient
Inspect response or GET /DetectedIssue for conflict alert
expected: MedicationRequest POST returns HTTP 201 with a warning extension OR DetectedIssue exists referencing the allergy
trace: R-HLT-059: alerts regenerated on conflicting MedicationRequest
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_allergy_and_medication_conflict_alert(
    clinician_auth_token, patient_id, penicillin_med_id, fhir_base_url
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}

    allergy_payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls",
                              "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]}
    }
    r1 = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/AllergyIntolerance",
                       json=allergy_payload, headers=headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    assert r1.json()["resourceType"] == "AllergyIntolerance"
    allergy_id = r1.json()["id"]

    med_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "medicationReference": {"reference": f"Medication/{penicillin_med_id}"}
    }
    r2 = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/MedicationRequest",
                       json=med_payload, headers=headers)
    assert r2.status_code == 201

    r3 = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/DetectedIssue",
        params={"patient": patient_id, "implicated": f"AllergyIntolerance/{allergy_id}"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    entries = r3.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "Expected at least one conflict DetectedIssue"

tests.name: test_duplicate_allergy_returns_409
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient resource exists, ID available via <patient_id> fixture
An identical AllergyIntolerance for penicillin already exists for the patient (created in fixture or setup call)
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with the exact same payload as the pre-existing allergy
expected: OperationOutcome body contains issue with code 'duplicate'
trace: R-HLT-059: Error pathway — 409 when exact duplicate
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_duplicate_allergy_returns_409(
    clinician_auth_token, patient_id, fhir_base_url, existing_penicillin_allergy
):
    # existing_penicillin_allergy fixture pre-creates the allergy and returns its code
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}

    duplicate_payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls",
                              "code": "7980", "display": "Penicillin"}]}
    }
    r = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/AllergyIntolerance",
                      json=duplicate_payload, headers=headers)
    assert r.status_code == 409
    outcome = r.json()
    assert outcome["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [i.get("code") for i in outcome.get("issue", [])]
    assert "duplicate" in codes

tests.name: test_missing_mandatory_code_returns_422
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient resource exists, ID available via <patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance payload omitting the mandatory 'code' element
Observe HTTP status code and OperationOutcome
expected: OperationOutcome issue severity is 'error' and references missing 'code' field
trace: R-HLT-059: Negative path — missing mandatory elements
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_missing_mandatory_code_returns_422(
    clinician_auth_token, patient_id, fhir_base_url
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}

    payload_no_code = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"code": "active"}]},
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{patient_id}"}
        # 'code' intentionally omitted
    }
    r = requests.post(f"{fhir_base_url}/AllergyIntolerance",
                      json=payload_no_code, headers=headers)
    assert r.status_code == 422
    outcome = r.json()
    assert outcome["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = outcome.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues)
    diagnostics = " ".join(i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in issues)
    assert "code" in diagnostics.lower()

tests.name: test_allergy_active_to_resolved_suppresses_alert
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient resource exists, ID available via <patient_id> fixture
Penicillin MedicationRequest already exists for the patient
Active penicillin AllergyIntolerance exists (created via fixture)
actions: GET /DetectedIssue for patient to confirm alert is cleared or marked resolved
expected: PUT returns HTTP 200 with updated clinicalStatus 'resolved'
DetectedIssue bundle contains no active conflict issues for the allergy
trace: R-HLT-059: alerts regenerated — resolved allergy must not trigger active alert
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_allergy_active_to_resolved_suppresses_alert(
    clinician_auth_token, patient_id, fhir_base_url, active_penicillin_allergy_id
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    base = fhir_base_url

    r_get = requests.get(f"{base}/AllergyIntolerance/{active_penicillin_allergy_id}",
                         headers=headers)
    assert r_get.status_code == 200
    resource = r_get.json()
    assert resource["clinicalStatus"]["coding"][0]["code"] == "active"

    resource["clinicalStatus"]["coding"][0]["code"] = "resolved"
    r_put = requests.put(
        f"{base}/AllergyIntolerance/{active_penicillin_allergy_id}",
        json=resource, headers=headers
    )
    assert r_put.status_code == 200
    updated = r_put.json()
    assert updated["clinicalStatus"]["coding"][0]["code"] == "resolved"

    r_issues = requests.get(f"{base}/DetectedIssue",
        params={"patient": patient_id,
                "implicated": f"AllergyIntolerance/{active_penicillin_allergy_id}"},
        headers=headers)
    assert r_issues.status_code == 200
    active_conflicts = [
        e for e in r_issues.json().get("entry", [])
        if e.get("resource", {}).get("status") == "final"
    ]
    assert len(active_conflicts) == 0, "Resolved allergy should not have active conflict issues"

tests.name: test_allergy_code_single_character_boundary
preconditions: Clinician auth token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient resource exists, ID available via <patient_id> fixture
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with a 'code' coding.code value of exactly 1 character ('X')
Observe HTTP status and response body
expected: HTTP 201 Created if single-character codes are valid per server profile, OR HTTP 422 if minimum length is > 1
Response does not return HTTP 500 (server must handle boundary gracefully)
trace: R-HLT-059: boundary — minimum field length for mandatory 'code' element
